study_title: Cardiocondyla obscurior Raw sequence reads
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Cardiocondyla obscurior: Old aged queen Gaster colony D_15
design: Examination of gene expression patterns in Cardiocondyla obscurior ant queens.Total RNA was extracted using the ReliaPrep kit (Promega). Spike-In RNA Variant Controls (SIRV-Set 3 Lexogen#05101 Lot 5746/001492) were spiked to a 2% fraction of the total RNA. Total RNA was amplified using single primer isothermal amplification (SPIA , Ovation RNA-seq System V2, Tecan) prior to cDNA generation. Genomics using Nextera XT sequencing on a NovaSeq 6000 platform. Reads were trimmed with fastpv.0.20.1 to a minimum length 70 and from Nextera adapters. cDNA libraries were constructed using the transcriptome library construction TruSeq RNA Library Prep Kit v2 (Illumina) and the SuperScript II Reverse Transcriptase (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
isolation_source: Laboratory colony JP 2011 (OypB) originally collected from Japan in 2011 (Okinawa, Oonoyama park).

design: Examination of gene expression patterns in Cardiocondyla obscurior ant queens.Total RNA was extracted using the ReliaPrep kit (Promega). Spike-In RNA Variant Controls (SIRV-Set 3 Lexogen#05101 Lot 5746/001492) were spiked to a 2% fraction of the total RNA. Total RNA was amplified using single primer isothermal amplification (SPIA , Ovation RNA-seq System V2, Tecan) prior to cDNA generation. Genomics using Nextera XT sequencing on a NovaSeq 6000 platform. Reads were trimmed with fastpv.0.20.1 to a minimum length 70 and from Nextera adapters. cDNA libraries were constructed using the transcriptome library construction TruSeq RNA Library Prep Kit v2 (Illumina) and the SuperScript II Reverse Transcriptase (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
isolation_source: Laboratory colony JP 2011 (OypB) originally collected from Japan in 2011 (Okinawa, Oonoyama park).

study_title: Transcriptomics of queen-worker pairs in slavemaking ants and its hosts
exp_title: RNA-Seq of Cardiocondyla obscurior Worker
design: RNA extraction of all samples was conducted in September 2017 using the Nucleo-Spin Mini kit (Macherey-Nagel). For each species, we analyzed a pool of 6 workers and another pool of three queens. We extracted RNA from whole bodies, as we had no prior assumptions as to which tissue differed most strongly in gene expression between our groups.  Samples were shipped to StarSEQ (Mainz) for library preparation and 100bp paired-end sequencing on an Illumina HiSeq. In total, we obtained 15 million reads on average per sample.
